name: Green Peppercorn Mustard
directions: In a blender grind mustard seeds finely.
In a glass or ceramic bowl, mix  together mustard powder and 3 tablespoons water and leave to stand 30 mins.
Blend mustard mixture and the rest of the ingredients until mustard acquires a grainy texture. If mixture seems too dry, add a little more water or honey.
Cover and let stand 12 hours before pouring into sterilised jars.
Seal.
Store in a cool, dark place 2 weeks before using.
Refrigerate after opening.

name: Tom's Fruit Dip
directions: MIx all ingredients together and whip until smooth.
Serve with fresh fruit of any kind.

name: Dry Rub for Barbecue Meats and Ribs
directions: Sift everything together in a large bowl, blend well using a wire whisk.
Stores for ages in a jar.
Sprinkle onto, not into, meat.
I added one tsp.
each of cardomom and cumin, and a half tsp.
of freshly grated nutmeg to a recent batch.
Friends that I have given this to, tell me they are using it on just about anything they put on the grill, from fish and poultry to steak and baby back ribs.

name: tomato butter
directions: mix all ingredients in food processor.
keep refrigerated.

name: Ham & Cream Cheese Roll-Ups
directions: Lay a slice of the ham on cutting board.
Spread cream cheese over whole slice.
"rollup" slice, jelly roll style.
cut this"Roll" into 1/2 inch pieces.
put an olive then a ham roll then a cherry on each toothpick.
put on platter and serve or cover and refrigerate.

similar.name: Hidden Valley Bacon and Cheddar Dip

name: Tortilla Rollups
directions: Mix together cream cheese, olives, chilies, and onions of desired.
Spread the cream cheese mixture in a thin layer on each tortilla.
Roll up jelly roll style.
Slice into 1 inch pieces.
Serve with salsa for dipping.

name: Garlic and Onion Dip
directions: Stir sourcream, mayo and sugar in a bowl.
Add remaining spices, stir till well combined.
Adjust seasoning to taste.

name: Balsamic Bread Dip
directions: Grind onion flakes and garlic powder into a fairly fine powder.
Add the remaining ingredients, cover with lid and shake.
To serve, pour on a (white) plate just enough of the vinegar mixture to cover 30% of the surface of the plate.
Pour High quality, extra-virgin olive oil over the rest of plate to cover the vinegar -- typically using two or three parts of oil to one part vinegar.
Note, the most important impact on flavor will be the quality of the olive oil, don't skimp.
Dip slices of bread in mix.
enjoy.
Keep unused vinegar mix in refrigerator.

name: Marshmallow Date Nut Roll
directions: Roll graham crackers to fine crumbs.
Combine marshmallows, dates, nuts and cherries Mix thoroughly with 2 3/4 cups cracker crumbs.
Add cream (not whipped); mix thoroughly.
• Shape into roll (about 3 1/2-inches in diameter); roll in remaining crumbs.
Wrap well in wax paper.
Chill several hours in refrigerator.
(over night is best) • Cut in 3/4-inch slices.
Top with whipped cream.
Yield: 12 servings.

name: Chinese Cabbage Salad
directions: Crunch up Ramen Noodles in bowl and set aside (the flavor package is not used).
Mix oil, vinegar, and sugar in small bowl.
In a large bowl, stir cabbage mix, almonds, sunflower seeds, and onions together.
Pour oil mixture over cabbage, stir well.
Refrigerate.
Add crunched up noodles before serving.

name: Mayonnaise
directions: In a food processor whirl together the yolks, mustard, salt and lemon juice.
Continuing to whirl, SLOWLY add in a THIN STREAM the cold oil, until you reach the consistancy you wish.
Stop processor and taste, adding 1 t. sugar if you wish, and whirl to incorporate.
This can also be done in a blender.
Variations-----------.
Add to 1 cup mayo 3 T.
or more grated or prepared horseradish for roast beef.
Add to 1 cup mayo 3 T.
whipped cranberry jelly and 1 T.
grated orange peel for turkey sandwiches.
Add to 1 cup mayo 2 T.
wasabi paste, 1 T.
light olive oil, salt and pepper to taste.
Whisk well and serve with crab cakes or grilled fish.
Add to 1 cup mayo 1/2 cup chopped herb of your choice and whisk well.
The sky's the limit once you start adding flavors to homemade mayonnaise.

name: Fresh Vegetable Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl.
Chill for 1 hour.
Serve with fresh veggies.

name: Spinach Salad Supreme
directions: Mix first 12 ingredients in a small jar and shake well.
Arrange spinach on a platter.
Top with egg and cheese.
Pour dressing over all.

name: Seafood Soy Sauce Marinade
directions: Combine all ingredients and whisk well.

name: Dill Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients; mix well, chill.
Serve with fresh vegetables.
Makes 1 1/2 cups.

name: Perfect Spicy Sausage Pasta Salad
directions: Cook Pasta in the usual way.
Drain.
Mix together the Pasta, chopped Spring Onions and sliced Beer Sticks.
Add Thousand Island Dressing and mix through.

name: Beer and Honey Marinade
directions: Blend all the ingredients and pour the marinade over your meat.
Marinate 1-24 hours in the refrigerator, turning pieces occasionally.

similar.name: Dry Rub for Barbecue Meats and Ribs

name: Lamb Marinade
directions: Combine all the ingredients and mix well.

name: Vanilla Extract
directions: Put the vodka in a sterilized glass container.
Break or chop the beans into pieces and drop into the vodka.
Cover the vodka with a lid.
Let sit on a shelf for 2 weeks, shaking every other day.
Use a tad bit less than you would regular vanilla extract.
When you are gettting low, just add more vodka if the beans still smell good.

name: Dip for Chips
directions: Whip cream till it starts to thicken.
Add cream cheese, onion juice and olives.
Beat until well blended and thick.
Refrigerate till ready to use.
Use with chips.

name: Easy Homemade Mayonnaise
directions: Combine egg, mustard, salt, juice, and vinegar in a blender.
Blend for 30 seconds or until well mixed.
Run the blender at low speed and add the oil very slowly.
Once the mixture emulsifies (about the last 1/3 cup of oil) you can add the oil more quickly.
Put into a jar and refrigerate.

name: Kentucky Steak Marinade
directions: Mix all the ingredients.
Marinate steaks for at least 2 hours but preferably longer.
Grill the steaks over an outdoor grill for best flavor (and to tempt your neighbors with the wonderful smell!).

name: Dijon Mustard Dip
directions: Mix all ingredients until smooth.
Cover and chill.

name: Easy Amaretto Fruit Dip
directions: In a medium bowl, pour in pudding mix- add milk and stir (just to dissolve the mix).
Fold in Cool Whip until blended.
Add the Amaretto and stir all together.
Keep cool until serving- use any fresh fruit for dipping- Strawberries, grapes, and apples work great!

name: Snow Peas with Shrimp
directions: Cover snow peas with boiling water and let stand for 1 minute.
Drain well and plunge into ice water.
Drain again.
Remove stem and strings from peapods, carefully open with a toothpick.
Combine shrimp with the mayo, green onion and lemon juice.
Fill pods with the shrimp mixture.
Chill and serve.

similar.name: No Cook No Class Shrimp Dip

name: KFC Chicken Secret Spices
directions: Place all ingredients in blender and pulse for 3-4 minutes to grind well.
Store in an airtight container.
Makes about 3/4 cup.
To use with flour, add 1 oz mix to 1 cup of flour for coating chicken.

name: Emotional Tonic (milk Bath Recipe)
directions: Add all the oils to whole milk.
Add the milk mixture to warm bath water.
Let the gentle aroma of jasmine work its magic on your senses.

similar.name: Cream of Anything Soup Mix Substitute

name: Hidden Valley Bacon and Cheddar Dip
directions: Mix ranch dip with sour cream.
Add bacon bits and cheese.
Mix together well.
Serve with your favorite chips.

name: Caesar Salad
directions: Mix oil, lemon juice, mustard and garlic well.
Combine romaine, croutons, parmesan, and pepper in salad bowl.
Drizzle with dressing.
Toss to coat.

name: Whole Grain Mustard Dip
directions: In mixing bowl, combine all ingredients; mix until blended.
Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.
Serve with French-fried potatoes or roasted potato wedges.

name: Cream of Anything Soup Mix Substitute
directions: Mix dry milk, cornstarch, soup base, onion flakes, thyme, dill and celery salt.
For equivalent of 1 can cream of chicken soup combine and heat 1/3 cup dry mix and 1 1/4 cups water.
For equivalent of 1 can cream of mushroom soup combine and heat 1/3 cup dry mix, 1 1/4 cups water, 2 ounces finely chopped mushroom pieces and 1 teaspoon salt.
For equivalent of 1 can cream of celery soup combine and heat 1/3 cup dry mix, 1- 1/4 cups water and 1/8 cup celery flakes.

name: Tortilla Roll-Ups
directions: In a medium bowl, mix all ingredients except tortillas.
Warm tortillas in microwave for 20 seconds to soften tortillas to make rolling easier.
Spread even amounts of the mixture onto the tortillas.
Roll tortillas.
Slice to serve.

name: Perfect Pasta Salad
directions: Mix all together well.
Serve, or refrigerate until chilled.

name: Chili Dip
directions: Just before serving mix all the ingredients together, place in an attractive bowl and serve with a platter of fresh veggies.

name: Spicy Beer Marinade
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.
Place steak (s) in a zip-type plastic bag, and pour marinade over steak (s).
Turn to coat.
Refrigerate 6 hours or overnight.
Grill.

name: California Oil Sauce
directions: Puree ingredients in a blender and store in a small covered jar.
Brush oil sauce on pizza dough, sprinkle on desired cheese, and then top.
Cook just like sauced pizzas, but they may take just a little less time until done.

name: Pea Salad
directions: Combine peas, cheese and bacon bits in medium tupperware container.
In small bowl mix Miracle whip and Sugar.
Pour over and mix in Pea mix.
Chill several hours or overnite.

name: Kitchen Spice Mix
directions: Blend all ingredients together and store in an airtight container.
Allow 2 tsp for a 4# roast; or 1/4 tsp for a 1# cutlet.
Add 1 tsp to a large pot of beef soup, or 1/4 tsp to 1 cup of meat or vegetable gravy.

name: Wholesome-fullsome Salad
directions: Place lettuce in bottom of a serving bowl, about 8" in diameter.
Layer mushrooms on top.
Then layer peas and carrots.
Arrange egg white slices and bacon over vegetables.
Top with half cup cheddar cheese; set aside.
In a mixing bowl, combine mayonnaise and lemon juice in a mixing bowl.
Spread mixture over top of salad sealing to edge of bowl.
Sprinkle with remaining cheese.
Cover and chill for 24 hours.
Before serving, toss to coat the vegetables.

name: Waldorf Salad
directions: In small salad bowl, combine mayonnaise, yogurt, sugar, and lemon juice, to taste.
Core apples, cut into bite size pieces, dice celery.
Add both to salad bowl, fold in nuts.
Chill up to two hours.
Toss before serving.
*variations: bananas& pecans or in the summer peaches and peanuts.
You can also add 1 cup of shredded cooked chicken.

name: Cappuccino Mocha Mix
directions: In a medium bowl, stir together espresso coffee powder, cocoa, nondairy creamer, sugar and cinnamon.
Store tightly covered.
For the label: For each cup of cappuccino, measure 2 to 4 tablespoons mix into a coffee mug and stir in 6 oz of boiling water.

name: Cheesy Ranch Chip Dip
directions: Mix ALL ingredients (except bacon bits), mixing really well.
Chill overnight.
When ready to serve, sprinkle with bacon bits (or you may mix them into the dip).
Serve with chips or veggies.

similar.name: Hidden Valley Bacon and Cheddar Dip

name: Dill Weed Dip
directions: Mix all ingredients together and chill for at least 4 hours in an airtight container to let the flavors blend.

name: Parsley-Dill Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small container.
Cover and chill for at least 6 hours or up to 2 days ahead.

name: Chipotle Dip
directions: Finely chop scallions.
Wearing protective gloves, mince enough chipotles to a paste to measure 1 1/2 tablespoons.
In a bowl whisk together all ingredients with salt to taste.
Dip may be kept, covered and chilled, 3 days.

name: Teresa's Dip
directions: Chop the olives in a food processor.
Combine olives with all other ingredients and stir until well blended.
Chill for several hours before serving.
Good with chips, crackers, and raw vegetables.
If you'd like the dip less thick, thin it with a little milk and stir well.

name: The Wedge
directions: whisk salad dressing ingredients together.
arrange a lettuce wedge on each plate.
quarter plum tomatoes.
add 2 tomato quarters to each plate, pour dressing over lettuce, sprinkle with scallions, and fresh ground pepper.

name: pasta with peanut sauce
directions: stir sauce ingredients in large bowl.
add cole slaw mix and cooked drained pasta.
stir to mix.

name: Olive Oil Bread Dip
directions: Pour olive oil into flat dish.
Add rest of ingredients and stir.
Dip your bread and enjoy!

name: Seven Layered Salad
directions: Layer ingredients in the order they appear in a flat dish.
A flat cake saver led works very good.

name: Mopping Good Salad Oil Dressing
directions: Combine the first 7 ingredients of the dressing.
Add the oil slowly and beat well.
Wonderful on pasta and tossed green salads.

name: Smoked Salmon Dip
directions: Finely chop salmon.
Stir together with cream cheese,milk,lemon juice,liquid smoke and pepper.
Add dill and serve with assorted crackers or fresh vegetables.

name: no-salt herb blend
directions: mix spices in a small bowl.
store in a closed jar for up to 6 months.

name: Veronica's French Onion Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients; beat until smooth.
Cut bell pepper (leave stem on) in half and scoop out insides.
Pour the dip into both halfs and use as a serving dish.
Serve with fresh vegetables, crackers, and/or chips.

name: Eight Layer Salad
directions: In large glass salad bowl, arrange spinach seasoned with salt, pepper and 1 teaspoon sugar.
Press down.
Add layers of bacon, eggs, and lettuce.
Press down.
Season again with salt, pepper and 1 teaspoon sugar.
Add layers of peas and onion.
Combine mayonnaise and sour cream and completely cover salad.
Top with cheese.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Toss just before serving.

name: Pasta House Salad
directions: Tear lettuces into pieces.
Place in large bowl.
Add remaining ingredients and toss well.
Let stand 30-45 minutes at room temperature.
(salt and pepper is to be added to suit your taste).

name: Coleslaw
directions: In a medium bowl stir, Miracle Whip, lemon juice, sugar, salt and pepper until smooth.
Add cabbage, carrots, and green pepper, toss to coat well.
Cover and chill.

name: Three Bean Salad
directions: Combine vegetables and enough dressing to moisten, toss lightly.
Chill and serve.

name: Green Pea-Goober Salad
directions: Thaw peas; do not cook.
Break peanuts in half at seam and add to peas.
Add sour cream and stir well.
Refrigerate 2 hours before serving.

name: No Cook No Class Shrimp Dip
directions: Mix together all ingredient and chill.
Serve with crackers or eat with fingers.

name: Zesty Seasoning Mix
directions: Place all ingredients in a small jar.
Shake vigorously.

name: Goat Cheese & Dates Crackers
directions: Spread a light to medium coating of goat cheese on the crackers.
Slice the dates into little 1/4 inch"O"s.
Place 3 slices of dates on each cracker.
Be sure to let everything come to room temperature before serving so you get the full flavor of the cheese and the dates.

name: English Pea Salad
directions: Combine all ingredients in a medium bowl and stir well.
Chill at least 2 hours before serving.

name: Basic Fajita Marinade
directions: In a blender, whirl garlic, soy sauce, and red wine vinegar until smooth.
With blender running, pour in olive oil slowly to make an emulsified marinade.

name: Red Wine Marinade
directions: Combine all ingredients, stirring until well-blended.

name: Earth Scrub (a green "soft scrub") all-purpose cleaner
directions: Stir liquid soap into baking soda.
Add the water, and stir to be as smooth as possible.
Add the vinegar and stir again.
Pour into a squeeze bottle.
(A clean, empty ketchup or syrup bottle works well.) Shake well before using, and keep tightly closed between uses.

similar.name: Dry Rub for Barbecue Meats and Ribs

name: Stuffing Blend
directions: Combine all herbs and store in an airtight container at room temperature.

name: Dough Enhancer
directions: Mix all together in a bowl and store in refrigherator in an airtight container.
Use 3 Tbs of this mixture in a recipe for whole wheat bread (3 1/2 cups flour) For white bread use 1 tsp.
For rye bread use 3 1/2 Tbs.

similar.name: Cream of Anything Soup Mix Substitute

name: Creamy Herb Salad Dressing
directions: Using a hand beater, whip together yogurt and sour cream in a small bowl.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
To Serve Cold: Refrigerate, covered until ready to use.
To Serve Hot: Gently warm over medium heat, stirring until heated through.
Do not boil.

name: Barbecued Popcorn Spice
directions: Mix and sprinkle over popped popcorn.

similar.name: Dry Rub for Barbecue Meats and Ribs

name: Ginger in Sherry
directions: Cut the ginger into 2-3 pieces of a few inches each.
Use a spoon to scrape the peel off the ginger.
You can also do this as you pull out pieces of ginger to cook with but I find it easier to do beforehand.
Put the ginger and sherry in a non-metallic container with a tight cover.
Store in the fridge for up to three months.
You can use the sherry for cooking and top it up as required.

name: Pumpernickel Dill Dip
directions: Cut the Beef into 1/2 inch squares.
Mix the Sour Cream, Mayo, Dill, Onion and Beef together in a bowl, best if done a day ahead of time to let the flavors blend.
Take one of the Pumpernickel Loafs and cut the top off, like you would if you were carving a pumpkin, and dig the inside of the bread loaf out to create a bowl that the dip will be placed into.
Cut the bread you dug out along with the second loaf into 1 inch x 1 inch cubes, to use for dipping.
Serve the bread bowl with the cut up bread squares around the loaf bowl.
Enjoy!

name: Nat's Sub Sauce
directions: Mix all together.
Spread on both sides of bread before adding your meat and veggies.

name: Tex-Mex Spice Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in an airtight container.
Sprinkle to taste on beef, chicken, potatoes, corn, popcorn, etc.

name: Laurie's Guacamole
directions: Coarsely mash the avocados in a bowl with the lime juice. Stir in remaining ingredients.

name: Just Celery Salad
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and toss. Serve.

name: (Non-Food) Cinnamon and Cloves for the Car  -  K
directions: Toss cinnamon and cloves into old nylon sock.
Put a rubberband around it and toss it in the back of your car.
Works better than the chemical car fresheners you buy at the store.

name: Mom's Vegetable Dip
directions: Pour just enough water over onion and parsley flakes to completely cover.  Let stand a few minutes until flakes are softened.
Mix together remaining ingredients.  Pour off water from onion and parsley flakes.  Add to mixture and mix well.

name: Taco Dip
directions: In a mixing bowl combine sour cream, cream cheese, and taco seasoning. Mix well.
Spread mixture into bottom of a 9x13 pan.
Layer the rest of ingredients on top.
Top with hot peppers if desired.
Serve with tortilla chips.
refrigerate if you would like to (and left overs).

name: Pesto Mayonnaise
directions: In blender, combine basil, spinach, cheese, garlic and nuts.
With blender running, add lemon juice and olive oil.
Process until smooth.
Place in a bowl and stir in mayonnaise.
Add horseradish to taste if you are using it.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Add diced sun dried tomatoes and stir just to mix.
Cover and refrigerate until served.

name: Ww 2 Points - Sunflower Caesar Salad
directions: Place the egg, buttermilk, yogurt, lemon juice, vinegar, garlic, salt, and pepper in a blender and pulse until smooth.
Place the lettuce in large serving bowl.
Pour the dressing over the lettuce and toss well.
Sprinkle with croutons, cheese, and sunflower seeds.

name: Ww 3 Points - Spinach Salad
directions: In large bowl, whisk the oil, vinegar, mustard, salt, and pepper.
Add spinach, toss to coat.
Divide salad among 4 plates, then sprinkle with bacon and top with eggs.

name: Save Your Pans
directions: Line the bottom of your pan, twice, with the foil.
Spray with cooking spray of choice.

similar.name: No Cook No Class Shrimp Dip

name: Denver Dip Appetizers
directions: Mix and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

name: Angela's Creamy Guacamole
directions: Split avocados in half (Gently sink your knife into the avocado starting at the "stem" until you reach the pit then rotate your knife until you have completed a circle cutting it in half, gently pull 2 halves apart).
Remove pits and use a tablespoon to scoop out the contents of each avocado half into a large bowl (use a bowl that has an airtight lid).
Take your knife and just criss-cross through the avocados chopping them very coarsely (they will break down more as you mix remaining ingredients).
Add chopped tomato, lemon juice, and mayonnaise.
Gently stir until mixed (do not over-stir!).
Begin adding seasonings in this order: Seasoned Salt, then salt, then pepper. Start with several shakes of each, stir and taste.

Repeat until you get it the way you want it. (It shouldn't be bland and you should just begin to taste the salt coming through which will bring out the taste of the avocados. It takes more than you might think but be careful not to over-salt since your chips will have some salt in them).
Seal immediately in an airtight container and refrigerate until serving.
If serving this on hot dishes, do not cook or heat the guacamole with the dish -- use it as a garnish.
This is best prepared fresh since avocados don't keep well (even with the lemon juice).
Enjoy!

name: Simple Foot Soak
directions: Mix all dry ingredients together.
Fill a large pitcher with warm to hot water.
Add dry ingredients to water and stir till salt and corn starch is dissolved.
Add honey to water and stir again.
Pour mixture into foot basin.
Relax for 10 to 15 minutes.

similar.name: Cream of Anything Soup Mix Substitute

name: Mozzarella Dip
directions: Mix all ingredients except 1 teaspoon of the peppers until well blended; spoon into serving bowl.
Top with remaining peppers.

similar.name: Hidden Valley Bacon and Cheddar Dip

name: Incredible and Easy Taco Dip!
directions: Mix refried beans, taco seasoning and 1-2 T salsa.
Spread in bottom of 13 x 9 inch pan.
Spread sour cream on top of refried beans - take all the way to edges.
Sprinkle corn on top of sour cream.
Spread guacamole on top of corn and sour cream - take to edges of pan.
Spread remaining salsa on top of guacamole - take to edges of pan.
Sprinkle chiles on top of salsa.
Cover entire dish with shredded cheese. Feel free to use whatever blend you prefer.
Sprinkle sliced olives on top.
Cover with plastic wrap and put in refrigerator.
Take it out of fridge approximately 1/2-1 hour prior to ready to eat. Serve with tortilla chips.

similar.name: Hidden Valley Bacon and Cheddar Dip

name: Growing Alfalfa Sprouts
directions: Add 2 tablespoons of seed to a mason jar with strainer lid.  Rinse thoroughly with cool water.  First day, fill half way with water overnight to soak.  Set out of direct sunlight and at room temperature, 70 degrees is optimal.  The kitchen counter is fine.  Rinse and drain twice a day.  At the end of day 5 or the start of day 6 your sprouts will be ready to eat.  Refrigerate your sprouts 8-12 hours after the final rinse and drain.  Enjoy your sprouts in salads, sandwiches, or as a topping to any dish.

name: Sandi's Special Seasoned Sour Scream
directions: Combine all ingredients and chill to allow flavors to blend (30-60 minutes) prior to serving.  Use about a tablespoon per taco.

name: Mangrove Mayo
directions: Mix together and beat well to blend.
Chill well in glass container.

name: Oysters Millionaire
directions: Arrange the oysters on a serving platter.
Cut the smoked salmon into strips about 10mm wide and 25mm long.
Arrange the salmon strips on top of the oysters.
Spoon, (or pipe) about half a teaspoon of sour cream onto the top of of the oyster and salmon strip.
Spoon about half a teaspoon of cavair onto the top of each.
Serve with lemon wedges to taste.
Enjoy.

name: Ahi Bruschetta
directions: Mince tuna with a sharp knife into 1/4-inch cubes, as uniform as possible.
Place the tuna in a bowl and add green onion, mayo, seasoning, sesame oil, sesame seeds, and mustard.  Stir to blend.
Mold the tuna in a small cup .  Serve with toasted baguette slices.

name: 7 Layer 10 Ingredient Taco Dip
directions: 1. In a 13" X 9" Pan spread 2 cans of refried beans.
2. Mix cream cheese and taco seasoning until smooth and spread over beans.
3. Spread Salsa over the cream cheese layer.
4. Spread Guacamole over the salsa.
5. Spead sour cream over the guacamole.
6. Spread shredded lettuce, chopped tomato and green onion evenly over the guacamole.
7. Top with shredded chedder.
Serve with Taco Chips.

Layers 2,3 and 4 can be rearranged to your liking, Any one of them can be left off and still be good.  When purchasing ingredients consider who will be eating this and gauge your hot/mild product selections accordingly.

name: Coconut Crab Dip
directions: In a blender or food processor, combine coconut milk, scallions, mayo, lime juice, and parsley.
Blend well.
Add crab or krab and pulse until combined.

name: Oka Ita (Tongan Ceviche)
directions: Chill all ingredients until cold, prior to making. Combine all the ingredients in a bowl and mix well.
Season and serve immediately.The ota ika will separate if not eaten on the same day.
Serve with additional chillies on the side.

name: Mayonnaise (1953)
directions: Combine egg, salt, pepper, sugar, mustard and vinegar in the bowl of a mixer.
Beat at high speed until thoroughly combined, about 1 minute.
With mixer running, slowly begin to add the oil, a few drops at a time.
Continue beating and adding oil very slowly until all the oil has been incorporated.
Cover and refrigerate.

name: Chickpea Cracker Spread & Sandwich Filling
directions: Coarsely mash the drained chickpeas into a bowl.  You want about 1/4 of it mashed at this point.
Add all of the remaining ingredients EXCEPT the celery

Give a quick stir and continue mashing about 5 more times.  Stir again and mash a few more times.   Stir again. We're not making hummus here so make sure there are chunks of chickpeas remaining.  Stir in the celery.
For tastiest results, cover and let it sit in the fridge overnight.

You can use it as is to spread on a sandwich, burger, crackers, on top of a salad, or whatever else you can think of.  This is a very versatile and forgiving recipe so use whatever spices you want.  You can use this recipe as a guideline and let your imagination take over.

name: Mrs. G's Best Caesar Dressing
directions: In a blender, combine the mayonnaise with the 3 tablespoons of grated pecorino, 1 anchovy and the water, lemon, vinegar, garlic, Worcestershire and Tabasco. Process until smooth. (note, you can smash or put the garlic through the press.  The garlic is boiled for 30 seconds just to take some of the "bite" out of the raw garlic, especially since there's a good amount of garlic.).
In a large salad bowl, toss the romaine with the dressing and season with black pepper. Garnish with pecorino shavings and anchovy fillets and serve right away.

name: Spicy Ranch Fire Crackers
directions: Place crackers in a deep, air-tight food saver container with a lid.
Combine oil, ranch dressing mix, and red pepper flakes and whisk together thoroughly.
Pour oil mixture over crackers, cover with lid, and toss gently to thoroughly coat; turn container periodically until ready to serve.
Best if made one day ahead of serving. Will keep in airtight container for approximately one week. Do not refrigerate.
